rulings.Case Title: IN RE: SANKAT MOCHAN HANUMAN LLC DBA DAHLIA HOTEL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Appoint Umpire
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 22, 2023 line 1. PETITIONER SANKAT MOCHAN HANUMAN LLC DBA DAHLIA HOTEL NOTICE OF HEARING ON PETITION TO APPOINT UMPIRE is GRANTED. Unless the parties mutually agree on an umpire, the Court appoints Hon. Charlotte Walter Woolard (Ret.). Moving party to prepare an order in accordance with CRC 3.1312. If parties agree on an umpire upon the expiration of the 5 days to object to the form of order, the name shall be included in the order. If parties, do not agree the name shall be left blank. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MHYF HOLDINGS, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL VS. CHRIS V. KELLY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 22, 2023 line 2. CROSS DEFENDANT SOTHEBY'S INTERNATIONAL REALTY, INC., NEILL BASSI DEMURRER TO AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend to address in good faith all issues raised in the demurrer, including but not limited to: how Cross-Defendant knew of the Cross-Complainant's lawsuit; how Cross-Defendant knew of the latent constructive defects; and how there is an actual controversy involving justiciable questions related to the Cross-Defendant's rights or obligations. Jolley v. Chase Home Finance, LLC (2013) 213 Cal. App. 4th 872, 909. Cross-Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice is GRANTED. (Evid. Code, 452)=(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: BLUE MOUNTAIN MORTGAGE, LLC VS. MICHAEL MARCH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Amend Judgment Ccp Sec 187 And 473(D)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 22, 2023 line 3. ASSIGNEE KELSTIN GROUP, INC. NOTICE OF MOTION TO AMEND JUDGMENT CCP SEC 187 AND 473(D) is DENIED without prejudice. No proof of service for attorney of record for Michael March. The Court notes that the declaration of counsel and points and authorities are somewhat inconsistent. If Annemarie was not to be named as a party to the action to begin with, the mistake to be cured is naming her as a defendant (Points and Authorities 1:26-27). If the matter with Annemarie was resolved during the pendency of the action (Solomon Declaration 2:4), then the mistake was the failure to vacate the default and failure to file a dismissal before the entry of judgment. The Court further notes that given the active default of Annemarie on file, no piecemeal judgment against Michael only would have been entered in July 2013 and none would be maintained at this time. Plaintiff does not request to vacate the default in this motion. The Court finally notes that based on Solomon declaration the issue with the judgment became apparent sometime in December 2022 (Solomon Declaration 2:3-6), but the assignee nevertheless obtained a renewed judgment against Annemarie before addressing the known mistake. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ALEXANDER JA VS. BENSON WONG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 22, 2023 line 4. PLAINTIFF ALEXANDER JA MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Triable issues of fact exist regarding retaliation and bona fide intent to exit the residential market under Drouet. Motion for Summary Adjudication is granted as to the following affirmative defenses (conceded/plaintiff shifted their burden and defendants failed to create a triable issue of fact): 11, 13, 20, 23, 25, 28, 29 and 30. Motion for Summary Adjudication is DENIED as to the following affirmative defenses (Plaintiff failed to shift their burden/triable issues of fact exist) 1-10, 12, 14-19, 21, 22, 24, 26, and 27. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: LAURA DARE VS. SVETLANA CHEPURKO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Response To Form Interrogatories - General, Form Interrogatories - Unlawful Detainer, Special Interrogatories Requests For Admissions And To Produce Documents And Property, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And Supporting Declaration
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 22, 2023 line 7. PLAINTIFF LAURA DARE NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSE TO FORM INTERROGATORIES - GENERAL, FORM INTERROGATORIES - UNLAWFUL DETAINER, SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS AND TO PRODUCE DOCUMENTS AND PROPERTY, MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES AND SUPPORTING DECLARATION is GRANTED, subject to opposition. Defendants shall provide complete verified responses without objections to Form Interrogatories General and Unlawful Detainer, Request of Admissions, Request for Production within 5 days of notice of this order. The inspection of the unit shall proceed on a mutually agreed-upon date the week of December 4, 2023. If parties cannot agree on a date, inspection shall proceed on December 7, 2023. Plaintiff to provide notice of such inspection no later than December 1, 2023. Request for sanctions is DENIED as it is not listed in the Notice of Motion. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: MYC ASSOCIATES LLC VS. JONATHON FERGUSON ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 22, 2023 line 9. DEFENDANT JONATHON FERGUSON DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint states a cause of action for unlawful detainer and is not uncertain. The additional argument raised in the Points and Authorities and not listed in the Notice or supported by any argument is also overruled: "The Complaint is Uncertain as to the Property's Address" (Points and Authorities 2:19). =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
